The Most Profitable And
Eye-Opening Journey Of My Life!
From: Todd Brown

West Palm Beach, Florida
What you now hold in your hands is a simple, yet unique and
wildly powerful resource. Because it hands you several examples of
real-world Big Ideas that have produced multiple millions in sales
and new customers.
Now, look: Before going any further, I need to make sure you
understand something. This is critical. So, please pay close
attention.
When I say “Big Marketing Idea”... I’m NOT talking about the
typical marketing “hook,” “promise,” “premise,” “theme,” or
“benefit-driven headline...” Nor am I talking about the “idea”
behind your product, your Unique Selling Proposition, your
positioning statement, or your string of marketing claims.
No. Those are all valuable. Sure. And those are all taught by most
marketing experts. Absolutely. As they should be.
But... as you’re about to discover... those are not responsible for
why some marketing campaigns produce millions a year while most
others don’t even come close.
Maybe you’ve been taught to focus on those other things listed
above... But, it’s not what makes an advertising promotion a grand-
slam home run. The Big Idea is.
It was only until I was knee-deep into my six-year journey of
mastering the use of the “Big Marketing Idea” that I came to a

4
The Big Marketing Idea
Formula™ Explained
Now, before I break down these promotions, it’s critical you
understand the Big Marketing Idea Formula.
Over the course of my six-year journey researching and studying
the Big Marketing Idea, I analyzed and dissected over 92 million-
dollar-plus marketing campaigns.
My aim was to see if there were any commonalities across the
board. To my astonishment, there were. Those commonalities
later became the foundation of what, today, is known as the Big
Marketing Idea Formula™.
Here it is:
E.C. (P.P. + U.M.) + I.I. = Big Marketing Idea
Looks awful, right? Like something out of an advanced algebra
class.
Fortunately, it’s much, much simpler.
Let’s quickly go through the formula. Then we’ll cover each piece
in-depth.
E.C. stand for Emotionally Compelling.
Your idea is Emotionally Compelling (E.C.) when it’s built on a
Primary Promise (P.P.), and when that promise is delivered by a
Unique Mechanism (U.M.).
I.I. stands for Intellectually Interesting.
Your idea is Intellectually Interesting (I.I.) when it piques your
prospect’s curiosity, gives them a feeling of discovery, and makes
them feel as if they’ve just stumbled on something newsworthy.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 30 --

5
Something… they’d be interesting in hearing more about even if
there was no Primary Promise (P.P.) or Unique Mechanism (U.M.).
Combined – emotionally compelling and intellectually interesting –
you have an idea which appeals to both the heart and the head of your
prospect. To both emotion and logic.
P.P. stands for Primary Promise.
The Primary Promise (P.P.) is one of the most important elements within
any marketing campaign. Because it tells prospects what they stand to
gain by engaging with your marketing message. It tells them how their
life will be transformed for the better by reading, watching, or listening
to your marketing message.
Remember, the main thing your prospects want to know is “How is my
life going to be made better by this?”
Your Primary Promise is what answers that question.
But there’s another critical piece - the Unique Mechanism (U.M.).
The Unique Mechanism (U.M.) is the unique piece, part, component,
aspect, process, or system behind your product or service which delivers
the Primary Promise for prospects. It’s the thing within your product
or service that gives your prospect the result. It’s the reason why your
product or service works.
Again, the Big Marketing Idea Formula™ is:
Emotionally Compelling (Primary Promise + Unique Mechanism) +
Intellectually Interesting
Let’s look at some real-world examples to see this formula in action.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 30 --

6
Swipe #1: The Marijuana Package
• Primary Promise: Turn fifty-dollars into a massive fortune
• Unique Mechanism: The $50 Marijuana Blueprint
By using the name “blueprint” we indicate to the prospect that this plan
is already done for them. It’s step-by-step. It infers that the prospect only
needs to invest $50 and make returns. Packaging a unique mechanism
with value-rich words such as “blueprint”, “method”, or “protocol” can
give the prospect the idea that the process is simple and won’t require
much effort on their part, reducing their resistance to taking action.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What is happening within the equity
markets regarding cannabis on November 8th that presents this unique
opportunity?
This promotion taps into the level of awareness prospects had at this
time… They saw that marijuana was being legalized in several states
and there was some talk of legalization at the federal level in the future.
Therefore, the buzz around marijuana as a commodity with high growth
potential (pun intended) was believable and interesting to the prospect.
Remember: things that are believable are not always true. Things that
are true are not always believable. Always strive to make sure your
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 30 --

7
promotion sounds as believable as possible to your prospect.
Also notice that this promotion uses a specific date to create urgency
right away, just before the headline. Often financial promotions will use
specific dates to create urgency, persuading prospects to read and take
action.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 30 --

8
Swipe #2: Canadian Social Security
• Primary Promise: Collect monthly checks of $400 - $4700.
• Unique Mechanism: Access to “Canadian Social Security.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What new and unique opportunity
now exists for Americans to take part in the Canadian Social Security
system?
Obviously, American’s probably can’t receive Canadian social security…
which is why you’ll notice the quotes around “Canadian Social Securi-
ty.” However, the promotion discusses an investment opportunity from
Canada that is very very similar to the way social security works, but if
it’s another stock, bond, or mutual fund - prospects will be bored.
By relating it to social security (the benefit of which is essentially free
money in your mailbox every single month) the prospect is now curious
and intrigued about what this opportunity might be.
You’ll notice that many successful big ideas use unique, controversial,
mysterious, or flat out WEIRD ways of communicating an otherwise
boring concept. THAT is one of the tactics that make a well-crafted big
idea so incredibly profitable.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 30 --

9
• Primary Promise: Slice twenty years off your age.
• Unique Mechanism: Medical science’s new “magic bullet” discovery.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: Has the “fountain of youth” really
been discovered by medical science?
As you can see, this promotion is a bit dated and likely wouldn’t perform
very well if we were to run it nowadays… That said, you still can’t help
but wonder what this “fountain of youth” might be, no matter what age
you are.
The promotion could be promoting a supplement, a fitness DVD, or a
water filtration system. It doesn’t matter. Because this promotion relates
the product being sold to a timeless classic (The Fountain Of Youth) it’s
instantly understood and extremely valuable.
Swipe #3: The Fountain Of Youth
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 30 --

10
Swipe #4: The New Railroad
• Primary Promise: The chance to become very rich.
• Unique Mechanism: The new investment opportunity in “the new rail
road.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What is this “new rail road” that
allowed an ex-bond trader to make so much money, so consistently…
and how do I get in on it?
This was one of the most successful promotions in the history of direct
response. It generated tens of thousands of new customers and generated
tens of millions of dollars during its life.
Many business owners and entrepreneurs knew that when railroads were
built to connect the United States in the early 1900s, it generated absurd
levels of wealth for railroad owners and shareholders, not to mention
created dozens of industries that are still alive today. The creation of
railroads represented the beginning of a massive economic boom for
America.
This promotion piggybacks on the economic explosion of the railroad
and connects it to “a new railroad” which in this case was… The Internet.
That’s what a big idea does. It takes something ordinary, boring,
or commonplace and mystifies it into something fascinating and
compelling… Something the prospect has no choice but to investigate.
This promotion is a perfect example.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 30 --

11
• Primary Promise: The chance to turn $10K into $474K in under 24
months.
• Unique Mechanism: A Swiss approach to capitalize on the coming
market shift.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What the heck is “Obamageddon,
what’s it going to cause, and how does it provide such a historic
opportunity to multiply my investments?
This big idea (targeted at a conservative audience) taps into one of the
most powerful emotions in marketing: fear. It also confirms their bias by
declaring Obama’s term in office an “Armageddon” while simultaneously
introducing the silver lining associated with it.
It also positions Obama and his followers as the enemy. Notice how
the second line mentions “smart capitalists.” The target audience
self-identifies as capitalist and accused Obama of being socialist or
communist. Creating an enemy or a negative force to fight against is a
powerful and deep psychological marketing tactic that can be highly
persuasive when used properly. This big idea communicates it simply
using just a few words.
Also notice the use of the words “Swiss investment banking.” This
positions the opportunity as highly valuable because the audience
knows how valuable Swiss money is, let alone how profitable investment
banking is.
Swipe #5: Obamageddon
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 30 --

12
Recognize the use of specificity in this headline as well. The use of
“between $10,000 and $474,030” gives specific gains on this investment
opportunity which creates instant credibility and believability. You’ll
notice that most successful financial promotions leverage specificity in
this way to create believability.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 30 --

13
• Primary Promise: Prevent debilitating physical trauma from diabetes.
• Unique Mechanism: The new information about diabetes care.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What mistake are good doctors
making with diabetes treatment… and is my doctor giving me this bad
advice?
The headline for this big idea leverages several extremely powerful
emotions in just a few words. Fear of death, fear of pain and suffering,
and doubt are immediately generated in the mind of the prospect.
This promotion is clearly targeted at prospects who have diabetes and
introduces fear, uncertainty, and doubt by saying there’s a mistake that
even good doctors make. In just 9 words, the prospect feels afraid, and
questions whether their own doctor knows what they’re doing.
Next, it mentions specific body parts that a diabetic typically worries
about or experiences discomfort: the eyes, kidneys, legs, nerves, and
heart. This adds additional credibility to the headline through specificity.
It lets the reader feel like they are in the right place, inferring that the
author is knowledgeable on their specific ailments and concerns. It is
not generic.
Swipe #6: The Diabetes Mistake Doctors Makewww.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 30 --

14
Swipe #7: Dark Trades
• Primary Promise: Collect $80K in a matter of days.
• Unique Mechanism: The anonymous trades behind every major stock
move.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: Are there really “dark trades”
happening before every skyrocketing stock price… and is it really
possible for the little guy to get in on it?
This big idea tugs at the curiosity we all feel for secret, hidden, under the
radar activity. It’s the same appeal that is made for any promotion that
suggests a “Conspiracy Theory”.
Furthermore, this promotion has an element of “insider trading” or
something under the radar happening on the “dark web” because it infers
that something is going on behind the scenes that they don’t already
know about. All prospects have a desire to uncover the truth, to discover
secrets, and know more than other people.
Again, notice the use of extreme specificity… 801.9% gains in 79 days.
It doesn’t get much more specific than that now does it? These add a
tremendous amount of credibility to the idea.
This promotion made a lot of money, but personally, if I had a chance to
improve it I would add a specific date when these Dark Trades will end
and perhaps a testimonial from someone who has successfully profited
from these trades.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 30 --

15
• Primary Promise: Gain robust heart health.
• Unique Mechanism: Spain’s military “secret weapon” for superhuman
health and wellness.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What did Napoleon’s spies discover
about how Spain’s military was able to create such vibrant and powerful
soldiers?
The first thing to notice about this promotion, right off the bat, is the use
of “Napoleon” and the “Spanish Army.”
You need to understand that the prospects who were shown this
promotion are older, conservative men who have an interest and
appreciation for history. Many of them might even consider themselves
“history buffs.” Aside from the fact that these are older men (likely
interested in preserving their heart health) the idea of this being
discovered by Napoleon’s spies is exciting, and intellectually interesting
to the prospect. The very idea itself has entertainment and educational
value.
It could be a boring herbal supplement they might have heard already
and would normally ignore because it would trigger their “seen it before”
alert, or what I call “mental opt-out.” However, in this case, it connects
the product to a much more interesting concept the prospect is going to
find interesting, educational, and maybe even entertaining.
Swipe #8: The Spanish Secret For Hearth Health	www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 30 --

16
Remember: when used properly, big ideas have the power to turn
something boring into something extraordinary that excites prospects,
making them much more likely to whip out their credit card and buy.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

17
Swipe #9: The “Secret Currency”
• Primary Promise: Gain wealth using the same method of the
wealthiest families.
• Unique Mechanism: The “secret currency.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: Is there really a “secret currency”…
and how have the wealthiest families used it to make money in even a
bear market?
Once again, notice the use of a “secret” that most common people don’t
know that powerful individuals are hiding from us... In this case, we
want to know what the wealthiest families are using to protect and grow
their wealth, no matter what the economy does. After reading this, there’s
no possible way the prospect will be able to resist discovering what this
currency actually is, and how they can profit from it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 30 --

18
Swipe #10: The Secret Bankers’ Account
• Primary Promise: Earn 30-40 times more interest on your money.
• Unique Mechanism: The bankers’ “secret account.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What is this “secret account”… why
has it been kept from the public… and how do I get one for myself?
This big idea taps into the injustice and victimization people feel when
things are hidden from them. In this case, rich bankers are hiding a
special type of bank account that gives 30-40x more interest than a
regular bank account, but they won’t share the information with the
public.
This big idea is an “expose” of previously secret information, which is
always a compelling angle because all humans have a deep desire to know
more than others and hate being kept in the dark.
Also notice how the pre-headline packs an incredible credibility punch…
It was said by the former accountant of two global banks “under oath”
indicating that what he said MUST be true. Notice how very few words
pre-frame the headline with an enormous amount of credibility.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 30 --

19
Swipe #11: The Golden Date Package
• Primary Promise: Earn 27 times more money.
• Unique Mechanism: The unknown gold trade to make now.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What is happening on March 31,
2017 that will cause gold to soar to $5K… and what is the trade to get in
on to make all that money?
The picture of the calendar with the date circled creates a sense of
authority by creating a bold and specific prediction. Anyone bold enough
to make such a precise prediction must really know what they’re talking
about.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 30 --

20
Swipe #12: Read This Or Die!
• Primary Promise: Avoid dying from a disease that already has a cure.
• Unique Mechanism: Uncommon information about disease
prevention.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: Are there really known cures for the
common diseases responsible for 95% of deaths?
This promotion makes a blatant appeal to the #1 fear many of us have:
fear of death. More importantly, this idea appealed to the fact that this
type of death from disease was easily avoidable, because there is already a
cure “somewhere” on Earth.
This promotion builds immense fear, doubt, and curiosity. The reader
wants to know which diseases these are, why you have a 95% chance of
dying from it when there’s already a cure, and where on Earth this cure
comes from.
Even though this promotion is older than dirt, you still can’t help but
wonder what diseases it’s referring to, and of course… the cure.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 30 --

21
Swipe #13: Retirement Plan B
• Primary Promise: Never run out of money when you retire.
• Unique Mechanism: Retirement “Plan B.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What is “Retirement Plan B”… and
how does it work to ensure I will never run out of my money?
The headline for this promotion (retirement plan B) infers uncertainty
associated with “Plan A”... simply by saying “Plan B”!
This big idea taps into the #1 fear of retirees: running out of money. It
offers a solution by offering a “whole new way” to approach retirement.
Also notice the faces of each person at the top of the page. Whether the
prospect knows who these men are or not, it indicates a high level of
authority and value because there are six experts represented who stand
behind this plan. It’s a subconscious way of indicating authority and
that the prospect will be receiving expert advice from several credible
individuals. Also by calling it “roundtable” it further infers that this is a
high-level, maybe invite-only event they’re now getting access to.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 30 --

22
Swipe #14: Trump’s Income Secret
• Primary Promise: Use the same secret income method Donald Trump
uses to start generating passive income for yourself.
• Unique Mechanism: Donald Trump’s “real income secret.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: Does Donald Trump really have an
“income secret”… and if so, what is it… and how are these other ordinary
people making money from it?
This financial promotion taps into a few core emotions. First, whether
you like Donald Trump or not, you know he’s a billionaire and therefore
knows a thing or two about making money. This promotion is using what
I called “borrowed credibility” because it’s using Trump’s name - and his
authority as a successful entrepreneur along with it.
This promo also taps into the belief many people hold about rich people
having sneaky ways of making and hiding their money with legal
loopholes.
Finally, this promotion adds considerable credibility and believability
with the use of several testimonials that include specific gains and
specific dates.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 30 --

23
Swipe #15: Social Security Loophole
• Primary Promise: Add anywhere from $400 - $4700 of additional
monthly income.
• Unique Mechanism: The social security website loophole.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: Is there really a loophole in the
social security website that will allow me to collect that much extra
money? How is that possible? And why can’t the government ever take it
away?
One of my favorite parts about this promotion is the image. By showing
a person on their laptop accessing the official government website for
social security, it adds considerable believability to the promotion.
If you’ve ever been on the social security website (or any government
website for that matter) you know there are endless links and pathways
to different sources of information and thousands of different options.
That’s why it’s very easy to believe that there’s some sort of profitable
loophole buried inside the website somewhere.
Again, notice the use of specificity… It says it only takes “three simple
steps” to cash in on “$400 to $4,700” in monthly income. This takes the
pressure off the prospect and saves them having to dig through the maze
of the social security website themselves to find the extra money.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 30 --

24
Swipe #16: 292-C Shares
• Primary Promise: Get rich from tech stocks with unusually high
returns.
• Unique Mechanism: “292-C Shares.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What are “292-C shares”… why are
tech companies giving a limited second-chance to get in on them… and
how do they work to give such high returns?
This big idea contains a classic example of what I call “neologizing.” That’s
where you create a new word out of thin air simply by putting quotations
around it. This is by far the easiest way to create a powerful Unique
Mechanism. It’s something that nobody has heard of… because they
invented the term.
You can’t help but read it and wonder WHAT a 292-C share is.
This promotion also does a fantastic job of creating immediate urgency
and a reason to engage with the promotion right now. The words
“limited 2nd-time chance to get rich” indicates there is a small time
window to get in on this opportunity. Also notice the use of specificity
in the examples given below the headline, comparing what the “292-c
version” of top performing stocks generate with the regular stocks.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 30 --

25
• Primary Promise: Make money from start-ups with this guy’s $80,000
a month approach.
• Unique Mechanism: “The 1,000% Backdoor.”
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What the heck is the “1,000%
backdoor”… is it some secret method for making money from
startups… and how is it making this guy $80,000 a month?
This big idea hints at a way to make massive gains by investing in
startups in a way known by few - hence the “backdoor” verbiage.
Another point of interest is the image. The man shown is a rather
nerdy looking, geeky guy. It happens to be the actual investor, but
his appearance lends credibility to the promotion. He just seems like
someone who is very smart. Imagine this same promotion with a
muscle-bound gym rat. It just wouldn’t have the same appeal, now
would it?
This is an important aspect of persuasion. If you’re promoting a product
for bodybuilders and you are the product creator, then you had better
look jacked. If you’re selling an accounting software, then you’re
best suited to be sitting behind a desk wearing a shirt and tie. When
used correctly, appearances can be an extremely powerful weapon of
persuasion.
Swipe #17: The 1,000% Backdoor
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 25 of 30 --

26
Swipe #18: 1 Whitehall Place
• Primary Promise: Get a piece of what made the wealthy elite so rich.
• Unique Mechanism: The “secret” of 1 Whitehall Place.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: How have the 1% of the 1%
wealthiest made their money… and what’s the secret of 1 Whitehall
Place?
This promotion piggybacks on the mystery of a specific address as the
unique mechanism. Similar to 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue (The White
House), this address almost implicitly says “elite”.
Think about it: Number one, the first, has a connotation to the
Presidency. Whitehall sounds like “white house” so again it draws a
parallel to the Presidency and the “Elite” class.
The writer of this promotion could have even made up the “secret”
behind this address, but it doesn’t matter if it’s made up or not. You can’t
help but want to discover how the richest of the rich (the 1% of the 1%)
got so incredibly wealthy… and you want to find out how they did it.
This big idea is simple, but as you can see, extremely powerful.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 26 of 30 --

27
• Primary Promise: Get a piece of this new $42 billion opportunity.
• Unique Mechanism: A new & huge money-making opportunity thanks
to new federal legislation.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What change to legislation has the
federal government made that has created this mega-opportunity… and
what is it?
In this big idea, you’ll notice a few elements at work.
First, notice the use of the Statue of Liberty in the background.
Although it might be overdone, it still communicates “opportunity” on a
subconscious level for most Americans.
Next, you’ll notice the use of all the zeros to indicate “42 billion”. It’s
always more powerful to use as many zeros as possible, when possible.
People like seeing lots of zeros, it makes the number seem bigger.
You even might find yourself counting the zeros to see exactly how
much money the promo is talking about. Now it feels like a massive
opportunity.
Finally, you’ll notice the use of the words “new” and “legislation”. This
makes the prospect wonder, “what has the federal government done now
to generate economic activity, and how can I cash in on it”?
Swipe #19: The $42 Billion Dollar Opportunity
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 30 --

28
• Primary Promise: Protect yourself from the coming economic event
with something better than gold… and get your piece of what was last
time a 665% return.
• Unique Mechanism: The secret currency of America’s wealthy.
• Intellectually Interesting Appeal: What’s the coming “biggest U.S.
dollar event in 50+ years” that the wealthiest Americans are preparing
for… and what’s this “secret currency” that gave triple digit returns?
This promotion packs a lot of curiosity, fear, and uncertainty into one
single sentence. Let’s dissect it.
First, notice the use of “America’s Wealthy”. This hints at the fact that the
elite class is always coming up with new and creative ways to generate
and hide their wealth. Also notice the word “now.” This indicates that it’s
a recent trend, so likely not something the prospect already knows about.
Similar to the other “secret currency” promotion above, this deepens on
the belief that the rich and famous have barely legal loopholes that help
them hide and keep their money. It’s secret, so naturally - you want to
know what it is.
Finally, this promotion creates uncertainty and fear by hinting at a big
“dollar event” coming soon. This taps into prospects’ existing belief that
the US dollar could collapse and gives them a way to secure their wealth
before that happens, just like the richest people in America are doing.
Notice how potent this headline is and how much it says in so little
words. That’s the power of a well-crafted big marketing idea.
Swipe #20: The New Secret Currency
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
